title: Enterprise outcome-linked amendment (branch)
thesis: Keep the flat site subscription as the universal base, but add an optional, bounded outcome-linked amendment for enterprise accounts only: a reduced base fee plus a capped share of jointly measured avoided-downtime value. Enterprise customers who measure downtime and accept availability KPIs self-select into sharing measured value; mid-market keeps the flat, predictable fee it asked for.

design.offerAndPackage: PulseGuard site agreement; enterprise opt-in outcome amendment with joint measurement protocol
design.priceMetric: Site subscription base; verified avoided-downtime value share on amended enterprise sites
design.pricingModel: Reduced base subscription plus capped outcome fee (enterprise opt-in only)
design.priceArchitecture: Hard per-machine annual cap, fee floor, annual reconciliation against jointly owned measurement
design.termsAndFences: Amendment available only where a measurement protocol is agreed; minimum 12-month term; cap and floor stated in the contract
design.migration: Launch flat subscription everywhere; offer the amendment to pilot accounts first at renewal
design.governance: Pricing committee approval required for any deviation from cap/floor; measurement disputes escalate to joint review
design.pilot: Offer the amendment to the three pilot accounts for two quarters before wider enterprise rollout

causalChain: Enterprise sites already measure downtime cost [EV-08] and accept availability KPIs [EV-18] → an outcome share with cap, floor and joint measurement matches both customer and Finance requirements [EV-18, EV-14] → value-linked pricing captures more of the measured value on critical fleets than a flat fee, without exposing NordMach to open-ended revenue volatility → mid-market is untouched, respecting its explicit rejection of performance-linked billing [EV-20, EV-21].
requiredConditions: Joint downtime-measurement protocol agreed per amended site [EV-17]; Finance-approved cap and floor [EV-14]; service capacity phased at one engineer per ~200 connected machines [EV-16].
failureModes: Measurement disputes sour flagship relationships; outcome fee under-recovers if downtime reduction lands below pilot levels at ordinary sites [EV-07]; administrative cost of per-site protocols erodes the incremental margin [EV-17].
smallestTest: Two pilot accounts sign the amendment at renewal with a two-quarter reconciliation; success = both renew the amendment and measured fee lands between floor and cap without dispute.

evidenceUsed.how: Enterprise customer explicitly accepts capped, floored, jointly measured outcome fees
Enterprise downtime cost is measured, making an outcome metric contractible
Finance requires cap, floor and measurement protocol — built into the architecture
Mid-market keeps the flat plant-level fee it asked for

contraryEvidence.why: Mid-market rejects performance-linked billing — the amendment must never be pushed down-segment
Pilot downtime reduction may not generalise; outcome fee revenue could disappoint at ordinary sites
Every amended site requires a measurement project, adding cost and delay

assumptionsIntroduced: Share of enterprise accounts opting into the amendment within year 1 (no direct evidence; requires a bounded expert assumption)
Outcome share percentage that clears the value story without breaching the cap (to be tested in the pilot)
evidenceGaps: No willingness-to-pay research on the outcome share level in either segment
No evidence on measurement-protocol cost per site
Only one enterprise voice supports outcome acceptance — a second account should be interviewed before rollout
